title: High-Availability Multi-Datacenter Configuration Management & RAG Arch
code:
graph TD subgraph "End Users" CMS_USER[Platform Admin] TEAM_ADMIN[Team Admin / User] RAG_USER[RAG Platform User] end subgraph "Network Layer" LB["Load Balancer (HA Pair)"] end subgraph "Datacenter 1" subgraph "App Node 1 (VM)" APISIX1["APISIX Gateway"] CMS1[CMS Microservice] OPA1[OPA Microservice] RAG_APP1[RAG Application Service] end subgraph "Data Node 1 (VM)" ETCD1[(etcd)] VAULT1[(Vault)] VECTOR_DB1[(Vector DB)] end end subgraph "Datacenter 2" subgraph "App Node 2 (VM)" APISIX2["APISIX Gateway"] CMS2[CMS Microservice] OPA2[OPA Microservice] RAG_APP2[RAG Application Service] end subgraph "Data Node 2 (VM)" ETCD2[(etcd)] VAULT2[(Vault)] VECTOR_DB2[(Vector DB)] end end subgraph "External & Managed Services" LLM["Large Language Models"] MANAGED_MONGO["MongoDB Atlas
(Managed Service)"] end %% --- Connections --- CMS_USER --> LB TEAM_ADMIN --> LB RAG_USER --> LB LB --> APISIX1 & APISIX2 %% App to Self-Hosted Data Connections APISIX1 & APISIX2 --> VAULT1 & VAULT2 CMS1 & CMS2 --> ETCD1 & ETCD2 OPA1 & OPA2 --> ETCD1 & ETCD2 RAG_APP1 & RAG_APP2 --> VECTOR_DB1 & VECTOR_DB2 %% Secure Connection to Managed Service CMS1 & CMS2 -- "Secure Connection" --> MANAGED_MONGO RAG_APP1 & RAG_APP2 -- "Secure Connection" --> MANAGED_MONGO %% Hairpin and External LLM RAG_APP1 & RAG_APP2 --> APISIX1 & APISIX2 APISIX1 & APISIX2 -.->|Proxies to| LLM %% Data Replication ETCD1 <-.->|Replication| ETCD2 VAULT1 <-.->|Replication| VAULT2 VECTOR_DB1 <-.->|Replication| VECTOR_DB2

ai.summary: A high-availability, multi-datacenter architecture for configuration management and RAG applications, featuring OPA, etcd, Vault, APISIX, and managed Mongo
ai.whatItShows: This diagram illustrates a high-availability, multi-datacenter architecture for a Configuration Management Service (CMS) and a RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) application. It showcases redundant deployments across two datacenters, including API Gateways (APISIX), microservices (CMS, OPA, RAG App), and data stores (etcd, Vault, Vector DB). It also details secure connections to external managed services like MongoDB Atlas and Large Language Models, with data replication between datacenters for resilience.
ai.whenToUse: Use this architecture for mission-critical applications requiring high availability, disaster recovery, and multi-tenancy support. It's suitable for systems managing dynamic configurations, policy enforcement (OPA), and AI-driven applications needing vector databases and LLM integration, especially when data locality or regulatory compliance across regions is a concern.
ai.howToAdapt: This architecture can be adapted by integrating different API gateways, replacing specific data stores (e.g., Consul for etcd, other secret managers for Vault), or extending with additional microservices. The multi-datacenter setup can be scaled to more regions or cloud providers. Policy enforcement (OPA) can be customized for various authorization models, and the RAG component can integrate with different LLM providers or vector databases.
